title: Content Intelligence Market to Reach $39.88 Billion by 2035 as Generative AI Transforms Enterprise Content Operations | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global content intelligence market is projected to grow from $2.65 billion in 2025 to $39.88 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 31.15%. Growth is driven by generative AI integration, hyper-personalized content strategies, and enterprise adoption across regions. North America leads with 40% market share, while Asia-Pacific is expected to witness the highest CAGR of 33.55%. Software solutions dominate with 71% market share, and media & entertainment is the leading end-use industry.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Adobe is highlighted as a key player introducing advanced GenStudio platform with Firefly Design Intelligence and expanding generative AI capabilities across Adobe Experience Cloud in 2026, positioning it as a leader in the rapidly growing content intelligence market.
Listed as a key player in the content intelligence market, benefiting from strong market growth driven by enterprise adoption of AI-enabled content solutions.

title: Document AI Market Surges to $27.62 billion at a CAGR 13.5% by 2030 | Report by MarketsandMarkets™
author: Marketsandmarkets
description: The Document AI market is expected to grow at a CAGR of 13.5% from 2025 to 2030, driven by enterprise adoption of intelligent document processing, generative AI, and retrieval-augmented generation. The BFSI sector and Asia Pacific region are identified as the fastest-growing segments, with major tech companies competing to deliver secure, governance-focused AI solutions for document automation.

title: Adobe's Strategic Acquisition of Rephrase.AI to Bolster Generative AI Video Capabilities for E-commerce
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The generative AI in e-commerce market is experiencing robust growth, projected to expand from $1.04 billion in 2025 to $2.44 billion by 2030 at an 18.8% CAGR. Key drivers include AI chatbots, AR/VR integration, predictive analytics, and 5G network expansion. Adobe's acquisition of Rephrase.AI in November 2023 exemplifies industry consolidation to enhance AI-driven marketing capabilities. North America currently dominates the market, while Asia-Pacific is positioned as the fastest-growing region.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strategic acquisition of Rephrase.AI demonstrates proactive investment in generative AI video capabilities for e-commerce, positioning the company to capitalize on the rapidly growing market and enhance its marketing solutions portfolio.

Launched Sidekick, a generative AI-powered chatbot providing personalized recommendations and real-time customer support, showcasing innovation in AI-driven e-commerce solutions and improving customer satisfaction.

Identified as a leading company in the generative AI e-commerce space, positioned to leverage AI technologies for enhanced personalization, supply chain efficiency, and customer experience improvements.
Recognized as a prominent player in the market, well-positioned to benefit from growing demand for AI-driven solutions in e-commerce personalization and customer relationship management.
Listed among key players in the generative AI e-commerce market, likely to benefit from increased demand for AI computing infrastructure and GPUs supporting the market's 18.8% growth trajectory.

title: AppLovin Pulled Back 16% in June. Is It a Buy?
author: Jeremy Bowman
description: AppLovin stock declined 16% in June due to broader software sector headwinds, despite positive analyst notes and no company-specific negative news. The stock trades at a forward P/E of 33 with strong expected growth (54% revenue increase and rising EPS), making it potentially attractive for investors who believe it can maintain its growth trajectory.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 16% in June due to sector-wide headwinds rather than company-specific issues. Multiple analyst upgrades (Edgewater, Raymond James) and strong growth metrics (54% revenue growth expected, forward P/E of 33) suggest underlying strength, but high valuation and sector volatility create uncertainty.
Disappointing earnings report in early June contributed to broader software sector concerns about AI disruption.
Disappointing earnings report in early June fueled concerns about AI disruption in the software sector.
Disappointing earnings report in early June contributed to software sector weakness and AI disruption concerns.

title: Why Figma Stock Lost 29% in June
author: Jeremy Bowman
description: Figma stock declined 29% in June amid broader software sector pullback driven by AI disruption fears, particularly after Anthropic's Claude Design launch. Disappointing earnings from peers like Salesforce, Adobe, and Oracle fueled concerns about seat-based SaaS vulnerability. The stock stabilized in late June following Citigroup's buy rating and Figma's Config conference announcements, with recovery continuing into July.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock fell 29% in June due to AI disruption concerns and broader software sector weakness. However, sentiment is improving with analyst upgrades and conference announcements, showing early recovery signs.
Disappointing earnings report contributed to software sector sell-off and heightened concerns about SaaS vulnerability to AI alternatives.
Disappointing earnings report contributed to software sector sell-off and investor concerns about seat-based software disruption.
